Implement quickselect with Lomuto partition and recurse only into the side containing index k, giving O(n) average time to find the kth smallest without fully sorting.
Run Johnson's algorithm: add a zero-weight virtual source, get potentials h[] via Bellman-Ford, reweight edges w+h[u]-h[v] to non-negative, then Dijkstra per node and un-reweight results.
Discard the first N iterations of any microbenchmark as warmup: JIT compilation, branch predictors, and cold caches inflate early timings and skew the average.
Fix late-binding closures in a loop by defaulting the loop var (lambda i=i: ...) or wrapping in a factory function to capture it by value.
Set separate connect, TLS-handshake, and read timeouts per request; a single blanket timeout masks whether DNS, the socket, or a slow server stalled
Set an explicit timeout on every outbound call (network, DB, lock); an unbounded wait turns one slow dependency into total thread-pool exhaustion.
Use `reduce` from `functools` to apply a function of two arguments cumulatively to the items of an iterable.
Tie every resource's lifetime to a stack-scoped object's destructor (RAII) so exceptions, early returns, and goto can't skip the matching free or close.
Compare two strings as multisets with collections.Counter(a) == collections.Counter(b) to test anagrams ignoring case/punctuation.
Use substr to check prefix/suffix match efficiently avoiding unnecessary copies.
Deploy a nonce-based Content-Security-Policy with strict-dynamic instead of a host allowlist, since allowlisted domains are routinely bypassed via their own JSONP or reflected-script endpoints.
Use maximal munch when lexing: at each position match the longest possible token (e.g. '==' before '=') to avoid splitting multi-char operators.
Call exec functions with an argv array instead of shell=True plus string interpolation; passing argv bypasses shell parsing entirely and neutralizes command-injection metacharacters.
For template engines, never pass user input as the template source itself; bind it only as data into a fixed template, since sandboxed engines still leak via attribute/method access chains (SSTI).
Detect stale handles into a recycled pool slot with generation counters: reuse bumps the generation so old (index, generation) handles fail comparison instead of aliasing the new occupant.
Give a reusable descriptor __set_name__(self, owner, name) so it derives its private storage key from the attribute name it's bound to.
Shrink a failing case by deleting or simplifying inputs/code while the bug still reproduces, until nothing more can be removed.
Use `pygame.event.get()` to handle events like quitting the game.
Precompute row/col/3x3-box used-digit sets once, then maintain them incrementally during backtracking instead of rescanning the board per candidate check.
Identify common characters and use generator expressions for concise counting.

When distilling free-text lessons from an LLM, filter output with a vague-language blocklist plus a concrete-anchor allowlist (backticked code, dotted calls, CamelCase APIs, Big-O) so platitudes never pollute retrieval.

Simulate decrease-key in Dijkstra by pushing a new (dist, node) tuple whenever you find a shorter path instead of mutating the heap, and skip a popped node if it's already in the visited set — the stale, larger-distance duplicate is discarded for free.

Guard recursive quicksort with a depth_limit (~2*log2(n)) that falls back to heapsort (introsort) once exceeded; without it, a last-element pivot on already-sorted input causes O(n) recursion depth and can raise RecursionError, not just run slow.
Decouple publisher from subscribers with an observer/event-bus callback list, but explicitly unregister on teardown or dangling references leak memory.

A 'no module specified' fallback that auto-loads every matching plugin DLL in the output directory can silently load mutually-incompatible plugins together; force single-mode by pointing it at a deliberately invalid-but-existing path.
XOR-reduce an entire list into one accumulator to find the single non-duplicated element when every other value appears exactly twice.

Avoid full-heap GC pauses by exploiting the generational hypothesis: collect the young generation frequently and cheaply, promote survivors, and scan the old generation rarely using write barriers to track cross-generation pointers.
Find LCA in a BST iteratively in O(h) by walking from root and moving left only when both targets are less than node.val, right only when both are greater, else returning the current node.
Support @deco and @deco(kw=v) on one function by giving the outer function a _func=None first positional param and dispatching on whether it is callable.
Use Fraction(*x.as_integer_ratio()) to extract the exact binary64 rational a float stores, then diff two such Fractions to prove the precise rounding error behind failures like 0.1+0.2 != 0.3.
For strictly-increasing LIS via patience sorting use bisect_left on the tails array; bisect_right would compute the longest non-decreasing subsequence instead.
Trace every return and exception path from a resource acquisition to its release; leaks hide in early returns or caught-exception branches that skip cleanup.
Compute gcd via Euclidean algorithm: repeat a, b = b, a % b until b is 0, then return abs(a).
Build Roman numerals greedily from a high-to-low value-symbol table with subtractive pairs like (900,'CM'), consuming num via divmod per tier.
Use regex alternation with the float branch first, r'-?\d+\.\d+|-?\d+', so findall grabs whole decimals instead of splitting on the dot.
Find the kth largest by keeping a size-k min-heap: push each element, pop when len(heap)>k; heap[0] is the kth largest in O(n log k) without sorting.
Review test cases to ensure correctness of logic not just output.
Rely on sorted()/list.sort() being stable (Timsort) so ties keep input order; sort once by the tiebreaker key to layer a secondary ordering without a tuple key.
Patch time.time or os.environ via monkeypatch.setattr/setenv inside a test; pytest auto-reverts them at teardown so tests never leak global state.
Build decimal.Decimal amounts from strings, never from float literals; Decimal(0.1) inherits binary imprecision but Decimal("0.1") is exact.
Model non-CRUD operations as RPC-style action endpoints (POST /orders/{id}/cancel) instead of forcing them into PATCH — ambiguous partial-update semantics invite silent state corruption.
For counting sort, iterate the input in reverse when placing elements from the cumulative-count array; forward iteration silently reverses same-key order and breaks stability.
Kadane's algorithm: at each step set cur=max(x, cur+x) then best=max(best,cur) to find max subarray sum in one linear pass.
Use collections.OrderedDict with move_to_end(key) on access and popitem(last=False) on overflow to build an O(1) LRU cache.
Grant standing admin roles rarely; instead use time-boxed just-in-time privilege elevation that auto-revokes after minutes, so a compromised account has a narrow blast-radius window.
Frame-count-based test scheduling is unreliable under variable frame rate (vsync, occlusion); give smoke-test harnesses a wall-clock scheduling mode instead.
Use generator expression for concise even number filtering and sum calculation.
Pass a function to re.sub so the replacement can inspect the Match object (group values, position, external counters) instead of only static backreference text.
Read the query plan for a seq scan on a filtered large table before adding an index; every new index adds write-amplification on INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE.
Attach line/column spans to every token during lexing, not just AST nodes, so error messages survive later tree transforms.
After n-1 Bellman-Ford passes, run one more relaxation pass to flag vertices still improvable, then flood-fill successors to mark the whole negative-cycle-reachable set.
Rate-limit with a token bucket sharded per (API key, endpoint), returning Retry-After and X-RateLimit-Remaining headers, so one noisy client can't starve others on shared infrastructure.
Centralize object construction behind a factory method when the concrete type depends on runtime config, so callers depend on an interface, not a constructor name.
Guard every untrusted length/count field against buffer-remaining before resize/allocate, or a malicious frame becomes an allocation-DoS.
asyncio.gather(*coros) runs coroutines concurrently and returns results in the SAME order as the input args, regardless of which one finishes first.
Wrap modular arithmetic in (a op b) % m; Python's % always yields a non-negative result for a positive modulus, so no manual sign-fix is needed.
Isolate each downstream dependency into its own bulkhead (dedicated thread pool or connection pool) so one slow dependency can't starve capacity needed by unrelated requests.
Ground an LLM's self-reported success by actually executing its code in a subprocess with a timeout and checking the exit code, instead of trusting the model's own claim.
Check leap years with y % 4 == 0 and (y % 100 != 0 or y % 400 == 0), not just divisible-by-4.
Convert camelCase/PascalCase to snake_case with two regex passes: r'([a-z0-9])([A-Z])' then r'([A-Z]+)([A-Z][a-z])', inserting underscores before lowering.
Build chunks with [seq[i:i+size] for i in range(0, len(seq), size)]; slicing past the end just truncates safely.

Implement topological sort with Kahn's algorithm (BFS on zero-in-degree nodes via collections.deque) so cycle detection is free: if len(output_order) != n after the queue drains, the graph has a cycle and no valid ordering exists.

functools.lru_cache needs hashable args (tuple, not list) or it raises TypeError; with maxsize set, re-requesting an evicted key is a fresh miss, not a hit, so check cache_info() before assuming reuse.
Use rfind to find last '/' or '\', then extract substring efficiently.
Use bounded retry with a guaranteed fallback for mutation/crossover operators: attempt N candidates, validate each, and always return an unchanged deep copy rather than risk an invalid result.
Acquire locks and rows in a fixed, consistent order across every code path so the database's deadlock detector rarely fires; retry the killed transaction rather than hand-serializing call sites.
Push a sentinel object through queue.Queue so the consumer thread knows to exit, instead of guessing when the producer is done.
Replace OFFSET-based pagination with keyset (seek) pagination: WHERE (sort_col, id) > (last_val, last_id) LIMIT n, so deep pages don't scan and discard skipped rows.
Use threading.Event with event.wait(timeout) inside a worker's loop condition so a setter thread can cleanly stop it instead of killing it.
After bulk loads or large deletes, re-run ANALYZE (or equivalent) manually — autovacuum/auto-stats lag can leave the planner using outdated cardinality estimates for hours.
Treat any non-zero exit code from a sandboxed untrusted child as a crash, not just timeouts, since it can't be trusted to signal failure any other way.
Choose UDP over TCP when occasional packet loss is cheaper than head-of-line-blocked retransmit delay, e.g. real-time voice or game state
Compute lcm as abs(a*b)//math.gcd(a,b), but special-case a==0 or b==0 first since gcd(0,0)==0 makes the division blow up.
